input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK (if not already installed)

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# If not installed, use winget:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# See what workloads are available (find the exact maui ID)
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android + Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify it installed
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Android SDK Dependencies

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths.

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was provisioned
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: check the dotnet maui-check or common path
    $androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Android\Sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK path: $androidSdk"

# List installed Android platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## Step 4 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Persist ANDROID_HOME so builds can find the SDK
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk

# Add platform-tools to PATH (for adb, etc.)
$platformTools = Join-Path $androidSdk "platform-tools"
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$platformTools*") {
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "$currentPath;$platformTools", "User")
}
```

## Step 5 — Install Java JDK (if needed)

MAUI's Android build requires a JDK. The workload may bundle one, but verify:

```powershell
# Check if java is available
java -version

# If missing, install Microsoft's OpenJDK via winget:
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify JAVA_HOME is set (installer usually does this)
$env:JAVA_HOME
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows App SDK / WinUI Target

For the **Windows** target, you need the Windows App SDK:

```powershell
# Check if the Visual Studio Build Tools or VS are installed
# Minimum: VS 2022 17.4+ with these workloads:
#   - .NET Multi-platform App UI development
#   - Universal Windows Platform development (for WinUI)

# If using VS, install via VS Installer. If headless, use winget + vs_buildtools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then modify the install to add MAUI:
# (or from command line)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
    modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
    --quiet --norestart
```

> **Note:** Windows target (WinUI 3) requires the **Windows App SDK** which is included with the MAUI VS workload. It also requires **Developer Mode** enabled.

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading WinUI apps)
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## Step 7 — Set Up an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install a system image (pick the API level that matches your target)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
Start-Process "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI"
```

## Step 8 — Create and Build a MAUI Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## Step 9 — Discover Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# Discover available .NET SDKs
dotnet --list-sdks

# Discover installed MAUI workload versions
dotnet workload list --versions

# Discover target framework versions your SDK supports
dotnet new maui --list          # shows available templates
dotnet --list-runtimes          # shows installed runtimes

# Discover available Android API levels
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Discover latest Windows 10 SDK versions for TFM
Get-ChildItem "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Kits\Installed Roots" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Get-ChildItem "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Windows Kits\10\Platforms\UAP" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
    Select-Object Name
```

---

**Checklist before you start coding:**

| Requirement | Verify with |
|---|---|
| .NET 9 SDK | `dotnet --version` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` |
| Android SDK | `$env:ANDROID_HOME` exists |
| Java 17 | `java -version` |
| Developer Mode | Settings → Privacy & Security → Developer Mode |
| VS 2022 + MAUI workload | `dotnet build -f net9.0-android` succeeds |

Save these commands and run them in order on your Windows 11 machine. Adjust the `net9.0` TFM prefix if you're using a different .NET version (the `dotnet --list-sdks` command will tell you).
